C68 PRIORITY B: SI #45 CIAP domain confirmation #3 — economic/market competitive gaming (third ontological type). Goodhart gaming by market participants: algorithmic trading adapts to regulatory detection (CIAP fails → super-linear), institutional investors adapt SEC reporting requirements (CIAP holds when reporting is fixed-schedule → linear). Prediction: algorithmic market manipulation = adaptive adversary domain; Goodhart Law in financial regulation = mixed domain depending on adversary adaptivity.

C68 PRIORITY C: Adaptive adversary ceiling characterization — exact super-linear form. CIA_i(p) = a - b×p (linear adversary response to detection probability). Ceiling = E[Σ(1-p)×CIA_i(p)] = (1-p)×(a-bp)×N = N×(a-bp-ap+bp^2). This is quadratic in p, not linear. Near p=0: ceiling ≈ N×a (no defense → maximal gain). Near p=1: ceiling → 0 quadratically faster than linear. Key result: stronger detection both reduces pass-through AND suppresses adversary CIA_i — double benefit not present in CIAP-holds linear case.
